title: (No AI) Suno leans into customization with v5.5
author: Terrence O’Brien
content:
![Suno V5.5 logo in big bold font, daring you to talk smack.](https://platform.theverge.com/wp-content/uploads/sites/2/2026/03/1774532259-suno_v5-5_blogouterbanner.webp?quality=90&strip=all&crop=0,0,100,100)

Slop yourself. | Image: Suno

Suno just released one of its biggest updates yet with [v5.5](https://suno.com/blog/v5-5) of its AI music model. Where previous updates focused mostly on [improving fidelity](https://www.theverge.com/24103840/generative-ai-artwork-suno-music-industry-musicians-copyright) and creating [more natural vocals](https://www.theverge.com/ai-artificial-intelligence/786349/suno-ai-music-generator-v5-review), v5.5 is about giving users more control. It includes three new features: Voices, My Taste, and Custom Models.

In the release notes, Suno says that Voices is its most requested feature. It lets users train the vocal model on their own voice. They can upload clean accapellas, finished tracks with backing music, or just sing directly into the mic on their phone or laptop. The cleaner and higher quality the recording, the less data is required. And to prevent someone fro …

[Read the full story at The Verge.](https://www.theverge.com/entertainment/903056/suno-ai-music-v5-5-model)

In the release notes, Suno says that Voices is its most requested feature. It lets users train the vocal model on their own voice. They can upload clean accapellas, finished tracks with backing music, or just sing directly into the mic on their phone or laptop. The cleaner and higher quality the recording, the less data is required. And to prevent someone fro …

title: (No AI) Why can’t TikTok identify AI generated ads when I can?
author: Jess Weatherbed
content:
![A screenshot taken from a Samsung video promoting AI editing tools.](https://platform.theverge.com/wp-content/uploads/sites/2/2026/03/Samsung-AI-video-promotion.jpg?quality=90&strip=all&crop=0,0,100,100)

Samsung, like many companies using generative AI in their advertising, hasn’t placed an AI label on several videos shared through its TikTok accounts, and the fine print doesn’t always contain the answers. | Image by Samsung

I've been struggling to tell whether the ads appearing in my TikTok feeds have been made with generative AI tools. As someone who spends a great deal of time scrutinizing images and videos for the usual "tells" that something was synthetically generated, some of the promotions I've seen have definitely sparked suspicion. For several weeks, I didn't see any examples with the AI disclosure required by TikTok's advertising policies, however, so I had no way of knowing for sure.

What irks me is that _someone_ knows for sure if the content is AI-generated. They're just not telling the rest of us. And if companies that [claim to support AI-labelling …](https://www.theverge.com/tech/885727/samsung-execs-unpacked-ai-deepfake-photos-vs-reality-c2pa)

[Read the full story at The Verge.](https://www.theverge.com/ai-artificial-intelligence/900400/tiktok-ai-ads-labels-samsung-disclosure)
